title: Repo: /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public Inspect existing latest-run reopen semantics in local kernel tooling (`k`, compact artifacts, active run logic). Recommend the minimal change needed so bare `kernel` auto-4pane behaves like the expected latest-run reopen contract. Do not edit files...
summary_text: Current gap: bare `kernel` currently bypasses the `k latest` stale-filtered reopen rules by loading `4pane-active.json` and resuming that `run_id` directly, then trusting it even if stale/abandoned. Minimal change needed: make bare `kernel` reuse the same "latest valid run" guard path used by `k` before `--run` resume. - [scripts/local/kernel-entrypoint.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/kernel-entrypoint.sh): in the auto-4pane/no-arg branch, do not `--run` straight from `4pane-active.json`. - Resolve candidate run by passing through stale checks from [scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh) / [scripts/lib/kernel-run-recovery.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-run-recovery.sh): - if run has no live tmux session or is stale (`is_stale_compact` age/absence semantics), treat as invalid. - optionally prefer choosing latest valid run (same as `k latest`) instead of trusting active file when stale. - If invalid/stale: skip auto-resume and fallback to fresh `--purpose interactive` (same as no active resume), optionally clear/reset `4pane-active.json`. - Keep explicit `kernel --run <id>` behavior unchanged. This aligns bare `kernel` with expected latest-run reopen contract with the smallest surface area change.
